title: feat: add builtin linker and integrate into compiler pipeline
author: euxaristia
body:
## Summary

Adds a builtin static linker for x86-64 ELF, eliminating the external gcc dependency for linking.

## Key Changes

- **src/codegen/ELFReader.ts** — parses ELF relocatable object files (.o)
- **src/codegen/ELFGenerator.ts** — rewritten with two-pass label resolution, proper .symtab/.strtab/.rela.text section emission using byte-offset name indexing
- **src/codegen/Linker.ts** — merges .text/.data/.bss, resolves symbols, applies relocations (R_X86_64_PC32, R_X86_64_64, R_X86_64_32, R_X

reviews.body:
## Code Review

This pull request introduces a basic linker and an ELF reader while rewriting the ELF generator to support multiple sections, symbol resolution, and expanded instruction encoding. Feedback identifies critical bugs in the encoding logic, specifically regarding rbp-based addressing, RI

The encoding for RIP-relative `mov` instructions (`mov reg, [rip+label]`) is incorrect.

Unresolved symbols are treated as warnings, and linking continues with their value resolved to 0. This will likely lead to incorrect

![high](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/high-priority.svg)

The `encLea` function only handles memory operands with 8-bit displacements (`mod === 1`). It's missing the case for 32-bit displace

![high](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/high-priority.svg)

The number of program headers is calculated incorrectly when there are no data sections. It is set to 1, but it should be at least 2

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

Using `number` to store 64-bit values read from the ELF file can lead to precision loss for values larger than `Number.MAX_SAFE_

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

Using `number` to store 64-bit values can lead to precision loss for values larger than `Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER` (2^53 - 1). Th

commits.author: euxaristia

title: feat(flatpak): run flatpak update on all upgrade paths
author: euxaristia
body:
## Summary

The initial flatpak support only covered the default no-op path and `-Syu`. Running `adapt upgrade`, `adapt full-upgrade`, or `adapt -Su` skipped flatpak updates entirely, creating an inconsistent experience.

Extend flatpak update to run after every upgrade path: the `upgrade` and `full-upgrade` subcommands, and the `-Su` flag path.

## Changes

**`src/main.rs`**

-   Call `flatpak::update()` after `apt::upgrade()` in the `-Su` flag path (upgrade without sync/refresh).
-   Call `fla

reviews.body:
## Code Review

This pull request integrates Flatpak updates into the system upgrade workflows. Specifically, flatpak::update is now called during both standard and full upgrades in handle_subcommand and handle_flags. Feedback suggests refactoring the Command::FullUpgrade logic to delegate to the Up

The logic for `Command::FullUpgrade` is now identical to the `full` path within `Command::Upgrade`. You can simplify this by del

commits.message: feat(flatpak): run flatpak update on all upgrade paths
commits.author: euxaristia

title: Add shell completion for zsh and bash
author: euxaristia
body:
## Summary
- Add `completions/_adapt` (zsh) and `completions/adapt.bash` (bash) so `adapt install <TAB>` and `adapt remove <TAB>` complete package names — closing the gap with raw `apt`, where the shell otherwise falls back to filename completion.
- Use the same data sources apt's own completions do: `apt-cache --no-generate pkgnames "$prefix"` for installable targets (`install`, `show`), `dpkg-query -W` (or `_comp_dpkg_installed_packages` when bash-completion is loaded) for installed-only targe

title: fix(build): detect Bun runtime in build scripts
author: euxaristia
body:
## Summary

`scripts/build.js`, `scripts/build_package.js`, and the husky pre-commit hook all invoke `npm` and `node` unconditionally, which breaks `bun install` and `bun run build` on systems where bun is the only package manager on PATH. The `prepare` script chain runs `scripts/build.js`, and the pre-commit hook runs on every commit, so this surfaces immediately when trying to set up the repo under Bun.

This branch teaches the build pipeline to detect the runtime and use the equivalent Bun co

reviews.body:
## Code Review

This pull request introduces support for the Bun runtime and package manager across the project's build infrastructure, including pre-commit hooks and build scripts. It adds a detection mechanism for Bun and optimizes the build process by handling workspace dependencies and paralleli

![high](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/high-priority.svg)

The Bun build path should respect the `CI` environment variable by running workspaces sequentially when it is set, mirroring the beh

title: fix(core): make shell tool work under Bun
author: euxaristia
body:
## Summary

Two related runtime issues prevented `bun`-launched builds of gemini-cli from running shell tool calls. The first failure visible to a user is an `ioctl(2) failed, EBADF` crash inside a React `useEffect`. After patching that, the shell tool stops crashing but every command returns empty output. Both stem from `@lydell/node-pty` not behaving correctly when loaded by Bun.

- **No output under Bun.** `getPty()` now short-circuits to the existing `child_process` fallback when `process.ve

issue_comments.body:
> [!WARNING]
> Gemini encountered an error creating the summary. You can try again by commenting `/gemini summary`.

commits.message: fix(core): make shell tool work under Bun
commits.author: euxaristia

title: chore(deps): replace image and rayon with smaller alternatives
author: euxaristia
body:
## Summary
- Remove `image` crate; replace with direct `png` decoding
- Remove `rayon` crate; replace `rayon::spawn` with `std::thread::spawn`
- Drop `rayon-core`, `crossbeam-*`, `either`, `image`, `num-traits`, and other transitive deps

## Verification
```
cargo check
```
Passes cleanly.

title: feat(runtime): pure-Rust runtime crate; port point.c
author: euxaristia
body:
## Summary

- Introduces `crates/runtime/` — a pure-Rust `staticlib` crate that will progressively replace the C runtime under `lib/src/` while preserving the tree-sitter C ABI.
- Ports `lib/src/point.c` as the first module. `ts_point_edit` is implemented in Rust and exported with `extern "C"` linkage.
- Wires the Rust staticlib into the existing CMake build: `point.c` is excluded from the C source glob, `cargo build --release -p tree-sitter-runtime` is invoked via `add_custom_command`, and the

title: feat(cli): show acknowledgment when user steering hint is processed
author: euxaristia
body:
Refs #26485

## Summary

When a user submits a steering hint mid-turn, today the CLI silently
splices the hint into the conversation and the user gets no visible
feedback that the hint registered until the model itself acknowledges
it — which can be slow, may be omitted, or per #21508 may even leak
the internal steering instruction back to the user.

This PR adds an immediate, fast acknowledgment line rendered in the
transcript right after the hint is submitted (e.g. `· Understood.
Focusing on .

title: fix: ensure closing fence in WrapCodeBlock is on its own line
author: euxaristia
body:
## Summary

`WrapCodeBlock` glued the closing ``` ``` ``` directly onto the last line of the content. When the input didn't end with a newline (e.g. a source file without a trailing newline), Markdown renderers never saw the closing fence on its own line and the code block stayed open through the rest of the document.

This appends a newline to the content only when one isn't already present, so inputs that already terminate with `\n` don't gain a spurious blank line before the fence.

Affects b

commits.message: fix: ensure closing fence in `WrapCodeBlock` is on its own line
commits.author: euxaristia

title: feat(bindings-cpp-ffi): add Rust FFI crate for WASM modules

Add `crates/bindings-cpp-ffi`, a new Rust crate providing type registration and FFI

existing tests are integration-level (WASM compile → publish to server). This
makes it impossible to test type registration edge cases, error p

title: feat: add bun runtime support
author: euxaristia
body:
## TLDR

Add support for running Qwen Code with **Bun** runtime for significantly improved performance. Bun provides faster startup times, lower memory usage, and native TypeScript support compared to Node.js.

## Dive Deeper

### Why Bun?

Bun is a modern JavaScript runtime that offers:
- **3-5x faster startup** compared to Node.js for CLI tools
- **Lower memory footprint** - important for long-running sessions
- **Native TypeScript support** - no additional transpilation needed
- **Drop-in com

reviews.body: The added `bun.lock` appears to be generated from stale or incomplete manifests. For example, it records `@lydell/node-pty` packages as `1.1.0` while the current manifests and npm lock use `1.2.0-beta.10`; workspace versions are also stale (`0.13.2` vs current `0.15.2`), channel workspace dependenci

**[Critical]** The standard `npm run build` path now unconditionally executes `bun install`, so users and CI environments without Bun fail before the build starts. I verified this by running `npm run
**[Critical]** `npm run start` still launches this wrapper with Node, but the wrapper now immediately shells out to `bun` for build-status checking, sandbox command resolution, and the child process.

title: fix: swallow resize() errors after PTY exit on Windows and Unix
author: euxaristia
body:
## Summary

- Silently ignore `resize()` calls after the PTY process has already exited instead of throwing `'Cannot resize a pty that has already exited'` (Windows)
- Catch `EBADF` errors on `resize()` when the PTY file descriptor is already closed and silently return instead of crashing (Unix)
- Prevents crashes on both platforms when a resize is triggered after process exit (e.g. during terminal cleanup)

Fixes #827

## Details

**Windows:** The native conpty `_exitCode` guard previously thre
